steps.title: Model the remaining corn
steps.content: Use time in hours as $x$ and bushels remaining as $y$. The bin starts with $24,000$ bushels, so one point is $(0,24000)$. After $5$ hours, $19,350$ bushels remain, giving $(5,19350)$.

steps.title: Find the line from the data
steps.content: Use the table points directly. From $(0,24000)$ to $(5,19350)$, the slope is $m=\frac{19350-24000}{5-0}=-930.$ Substituting a point into $y=mx+b$ gives $b=24000$. The Desmos rows graph the model and the target amount, $12,840$ bushels; their intersection gives the time.

steps.title: Find when $12,840$ remains
steps.content: Set the model equal to $12,840$: $$-930x+24000=12840.$$ Then $11160=930x$, so $x=12$. The total time is 12 hours.
